---
title: "Predict with an Existing Model"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Predict with an Existing Model}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Use this workflow when you already have an exported model bundle.

## Step 1. Create a prediction spec

```r
spec <- create_spec(
  predict = list(
    model_bundle = "path/to/exported_bundle"
  ),
  labels = list(
    path = NULL
  )
)
```

## Step 2. Run prediction

```r
run_pipeline(spec, stage = "predict")
```

The default output is an epoch-level predictions file written into the run's
`results/` folder.

## Optional summaries

You can also request summaries:

```r
spec <- create_spec(
  predict = list(
    model_bundle = "path/to/exported_bundle",
    summary_outputs = c("hourly", "daily")
  ),
  labels = list(path = NULL)
)
```

## Beginner option

```r
wizard_predict()
```
